title: A Roupa e a Moda
subtitle: Clothing, Fashion & Connectors
description: Learn clothing vocabulary, describe outfits, express how long things have been happening with HÁ/DESDE, and connect ideas with conjunctions.

vocabulary.example: Preciso de comprar roupa nova para o verão.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I need to buy new clothes for summer.
vocabulary.tip: Collective noun — always singular: 'a roupa' (clothing). For individual items, use specific words: 'a camisola', 'as calças'.

vocabulary.example: Esta camisola azul fica-te muito bem.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: This blue jumper suits you very well.
vocabulary.tip: In EP, 'camisola' = sweater/jumper/T-shirt. 'A camisa' = dress shirt (button-up). Don't confuse them!

vocabulary.example: Estas calças pretas são muito elegantes.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: These black trousers are very elegant.
vocabulary.tip: Always plural in Portuguese: 'as calças' (the trousers). 'Calças de ganga' = jeans. 'Um par de calças' = a pair of trousers.

vocabulary.example: A Sofia usa um vestido vermelho na festa.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Sofia wears a red dress at the party.
vocabulary.tip: 'Vestido de noite' = evening dress, 'vestido de verão' = summer dress. The verb 'vestir' = to dress/wear.

vocabulary.example: Prefiro saias a calças no verão.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I prefer skirts to trousers in summer.
vocabulary.tip: 'Saia curta' = short skirt, 'saia comprida' = long skirt. 'Uma saia plissada' = a pleated skirt.

vocabulary.example: Estes sapatos são muito confortáveis para caminhar.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: These shoes are very comfortable for walking.
vocabulary.tip: Always plural. 'Um par de sapatos' (a pair of shoes). 'Sapatos de salto alto' = high heels. 'Sapatos rasos' = flat shoes.

vocabulary.example: No inverno uso sempre as minhas botas castanhas.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: In winter I always wear my brown boots.
vocabulary.tip: 'Botas de chuva' = rain boots/wellies. 'Botas de montanha' = hiking boots.

vocabulary.example: Não te esqueças do casaco — está frio.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Don't forget your coat — it's cold.
vocabulary.tip: 'Casaco de malha' = cardigan/knit jacket. 'Casaco de chuva' = rain jacket. 'Sobretudo' = overcoat.

vocabulary.example: Usa um chapéu quando está muito sol.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Wear a hat when it's very sunny.
vocabulary.tip: 'Chapéu de sol' = sun hat. 'Boné' = baseball cap. 'Gorro' = beanie/woollen hat.

vocabulary.example: No inverno uso cachecol e luvas quando saio.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: In winter I wear a scarf and gloves when I go out.
vocabulary.tip: 'Cachecol' = scarf (long, wrapped). 'Lenço' = handkerchief or small neck scarf.

vocabulary.example: Esqueci as luvas em casa e tenho as mãos frias.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I forgot my gloves at home and my hands are cold.
vocabulary.tip: Always plural. 'Um par de luvas' = a pair of gloves. 'Luvas de lã' = wool gloves.

vocabulary.example: Hoje vou usar um vestido azul. Visto sempre roupa confortável.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Today I'm going to wear a blue dress. I always wear comfortable clothes.
vocabulary.tip: 'Usar' = to wear (habitually/generally). 'Vestir' = to put on clothing (action). Both are common in EP.

vocabulary.example: Essa cor fica-te muito bem!
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: That colour suits you very well!
vocabulary.tip: 'Ficar bem/mal' with indirect object pronoun: 'fica-me bem' (it suits me), 'fica-te bem' (it suits you).

vocabulary.example: Qual é a tua cor favorita para roupa?
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: What is your favourite colour for clothing?
vocabulary.tip: Colours agree with nouns: 'um vestido vermelho' (m), 'uma camisa vermelha' (f). Exception: 'rosa, laranja, azul-marinho' are invariable.

vocabulary.example: Tem este casaco em tamanho médio?
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Do you have this coat in medium size?
vocabulary.tip: Sizes: 'pequeno' (small/S), 'médio' (medium/M), 'grande' (large/L), 'extra grande' (XL).

vocabulary.example: Posso experimentar este vestido?
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Can I try on this dress?
vocabulary.tip: Regular -ar verb. 'O/A provador/a' = changing room/fitting room. 'Caber' = to fit (in size).

vocabulary.example: Moro em Lisboa há dois anos.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I have been living in Lisbon for two years.
vocabulary.tip: 'Há + time' expresses duration up to now: 'Estudo português há seis meses' = I've been studying Portuguese for 6 months.

vocabulary.example: Trabalho aqui desde janeiro.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I have been working here since January.
vocabulary.tip: 'Desde' + specific point: 'desde segunda-feira' (since Monday), 'desde 2020'. Contrast with 'há' + duration: 'há três dias' (for three days).

vocabulary.example: Quando está frio, uso sempre um casaco.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: When it's cold, I always wear a coat.
vocabulary.tip: 'Quando' introduces a time clause. Also used as a question word: 'Quando chegaste?' (When did you arrive?)

vocabulary.example: Gosto desta loja porque tem preços bons.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I like this shop because it has good prices.
vocabulary.tip: 'Porque' (one word, unstressed) = because. 'Por quê?' / 'Porquê?' = why? (question). Don't confuse them!

vocabulary.example: Gosto deste vestido, mas é muito caro.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I like this dress, but it's very expensive.
vocabulary.tip: 'Mas' = but (contrasting ideas). 'Porém' and 'contudo' are more formal alternatives.

vocabulary.example: Vou comprar umas calças e uma camisola.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I'm going to buy some trousers and a jumper.
vocabulary.tip: 'E' = and. Before words starting with 'e' sound, it can change to 'é': rare. Very common connector in everyday speech.

vocabulary.translation: to me / to you / to him/her
vocabulary.example: Podes dar-me uma ajuda? Ela deu-lhe um presente.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Can you give me a hand? She gave him/her a present.
vocabulary.tip: Indirect object pronouns come after the verb with a hyphen in EP: 'deu-me' (gave me), 'mostrou-te' (showed you).

sections.heading: HÁ vs DESDE — Duration and Starting Point
sections.body: Two very useful time expressions in Portuguese: (1) 'HÁ + time period' expresses how long something has been happening (duration up to now): 'Moro em Portugal há dois anos.' (I have been living in Portugal for two years.) (2) 'DESDE + specific moment' expresses since when something has been happening: 'Moro em Portugal desde março.' (I have been living in Portugal since March.) The big difference: 'há' uses a time period (two years, three months), 'desde' uses a specific point (March, 2020, Monday).

sections.heading: HÁ vs DESDE — Examples
rows: Estudo aqui há seis meses. (I've studied here for 6 months.)
Estudo aqui desde setembro. (I've studied here since September.)

sections.heading: Indirect Object Pronouns
sections.body: Indirect object pronouns tell us TO WHOM or FOR WHOM something is done. In European Portuguese, these pronouns typically come after the verb, joined with a hyphen: 'Ela deu-me um livro.' (She gave me a book.) 'Posso mostrar-te este vestido?' (Can I show you this dress?) The forms are: me (to me), te (to you/informal), lhe (to him/her/você), nos (to us), vos (to you all/formal), lhes (to them/vocês).

sections.heading: Indirect Object Pronouns
rows: Ela deu-me um presente. (She gave me a gift.)
Vou mostrar-te algo especial. (I'll show you something special.)
Comprei-lhe umas calças novas. (I bought him/her new trousers.)
O professor explicou-nos a gramática. (The teacher explained grammar to us.)
Enviei-lhes uma mensagem. (I sent them a message.)

sections.heading: Connectors — Building Complex Sentences
sections.body: Connectors link ideas together. The four key connectors at A1 level are: (1) 'e' (and) — adds information: 'Gosto de calças e saias.' (2) 'mas' (but) — contrasts information: 'É bonito mas é caro.' (3) 'porque' (because) — gives a reason: 'Comprei porque estava em promoção.' (4) 'quando' (when) — introduces a time condition: 'Quando está frio, uso um casaco.' Using these connectors will make your Portuguese sound much more natural.

sections.heading: The Four Key Connectors
rows: Gosto de calças e de saias.

items.portuguese: Estudo português há três meses e já falo um pouco.
items.translation: I've been studying Portuguese for three months and I already speak a little.
items.pronunciation: esh-TOO-doh por-too-GAYSH ah trehsh MEH-zesh ee zhah FAH-loo oom POO-koo

items.portuguese: Gosto deste casaco, mas não tenho dinheiro suficiente.
items.translation: I like this coat, but I don't have enough money.

items.portuguese: Moro em Portugal desde setembro porque gosto do clima.
items.translation: I've been living in Portugal since September because I like the climate.

items.portuguese: Quando está frio, visto sempre um casaco e uso cachecol.
items.translation: When it's cold, I always put on a coat and wear a scarf.

sections.heading: Common Mistakes & Tips
items: HÁ vs DESDE: 'há dois anos' (for two years — duration) vs 'desde 2022' (since 2022 — starting point). Use 'há' with time periods, 'desde' with dates/moments.
In EP, indirect object pronouns follow the verb with a hyphen: 'deu-me', 'mostrou-te'. In questions or with negation, the pronoun can come before: 'Não me deu nada.'
'Porque' (because) vs 'Porquê?' (Why?): 'Não fui porque estava doente.' (I didn't go because I was ill.) 'Porquê?' (Why?)
Clothing is always discussed with the verb USAR (to wear/use): 'Uso calças' (I wear trousers), NOT 'Porto calças' (that's more literary).
Remember colour agreement: 'um casaco azul' (masculine), 'uma saia azul' (feminine) — but most colours agree: 'vermelho/vermelha', 'branco/branca'.

items.question: Moro em Lisboa __ dois anos.
items.explanation: 'Há + time period' expresses duration: 'há dois anos' = for two years (up until now).

items.question: __ quanto tempo estudas português?
items.explanation: 'Há quanto tempo?' = how long? (asking about duration). 'Desde quando?' = since when?

items.question: Gosto deste vestido __ é muito caro.
items.explanation: 'Mas' = but (contrast). 'I like this dress but it's very expensive' — contrasting two ideas.

exercises.title: Clothing and Connectors
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct word.

items.fullAnswer: Posso experimentar este vestido, por favor?

items.sentence: Gosto de usar saias __ calças no verão.
items.fullAnswer: Gosto de usar saias e calças no verão.

items.sentence: Ela deu-__ um casaco azul de presente.
items.fullAnswer: Ela deu-me um casaco azul de presente.

exercises.title: Build Sentences with Connectors
exercises.instructions: Click the correct word to complete each sentence.

items.sentence: Não comprei o casaco ___ era muito caro.
Quando está frio, ___ sempre um casaco.
